info.title: memgit Memory API
info.description: Version-controlled persistent memory store for AI assistants. Stores facts, rules, preferences, and lessons learned across sessions. Use search_memories at the start of every session to retrieve relevant context. Use save_memory whenever you learn something worth remembering for future sessions.

servers.description: Local memgit HTTP server (run: memgit serve --http)

post.description: Search the persistent memory store for facts, rules, and lessons relevant to the current task. CALL THIS at the start of every session, before answering questions about past work, before applying preferences the user may have stated before, or whenever you are unsure whether you have prior context on a topic. Returns memories ranked by BM25 relevance — only what matters, not everything.

query.description: Natural-language description of what you want to recall. Examples: 'user preferences for code style', 'how does the Instagram pipeline work', 'what trading rules should I follow'

top_k.description: Max results to return. Use 15-20 for broad topic discovery.

type_filter.description: fb=feedback (corrections, preferences), us=user profile (role, goals), pj=project (active work, decisions), rf=reference (pointers to external systems), cn=convention (code/architecture rules), lx=lesson (post-mortems, 'we got burned by X')

get.summary: Get a single memory by slug
get.description: Fetch a single memory by its exact slug identifier. CALL THIS when search_memories returned a relevant slug and you need the full details (why, when to apply, tags).

put.description: Persist a new fact, rule, preference, or lesson to the memory store for future sessions. CALL THIS whenever you learn something the user would want you to remember next time — a preference they stated, a rule they corrected you on, a project decision, a lesson from a mistake, or a reference to an external system. Do NOT save ephemeral or task-specific details. If a memory with the same slug already exists, this updates it.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: Short kebab-case identifier, unique per memory. Examples: 'user-prefers-terse-responses', 'trading-confirm-only-orders'

rule.description: The primary fact or rule to remember. Write as a declarative statement: 'always X', 'never Y', 'Z is located at ...'

type_code.description: fb=feedback, us=user, pj=project, rf=reference, cn=convention, lx=lesson

why.description: Why this rule exists — the incident, reason, or motivation. Optional but strongly recommended.

when.description: When or where to apply this rule.

get.description: List all memories, returning slug + rule for each. CALL THIS to browse what is stored or discover memory slugs for get_memory. For finding relevant memories on a topic, use search_memories instead — it is faster and ranked.

parameters.name: type_filter
parameters.description: Filter by type: fb=feedback, us=user, pj=project, rf=reference, cn=convention, lx=lesson

parameters.name: min_priority
parameters.description: Only return memories at or above this priority (1=all, 2=medium+, 3=critical only)

get.description: Show recent checkpoint history — when memories were last synced and what changed. CALL THIS to understand how fresh the memory store is.

type.description: fb=feedback, us=user, pj=project, rf=reference, cn=convention, lx=lesson

score.description: BM25 relevance score (only present in search results)
